revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Furutsu Hachimanyama Site]][[ja:古津八幡山遺跡]]
{{Short description|Yayoi period archaeological site in Akiha-ku, Hokuriku, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Furutsu Hachimanyama Site | native_name =古津八幡山遺跡 | native_name_lang = | image = NiigataCityOpenData rekishinohiroba02.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Furutsu Hachimanyama kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Furutsu Hachimanyama Site | map_size = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|45|50|N|139|06|55|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Akiha-ku, Niigata|en|Akiha-ku, Niigata|qid=Q711733|lt=Akiha-ku, Niigata|1=Akiha-ku, Niigata}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = settlement, kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park, Museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Furutsu Hachimanyama Site'''|古津八幡山遺跡|Furutsu Hachimanyama iseki}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|qid=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} containing the remnants of a late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} moated settlement with three ''[[Kofun]]'' tumuli located in the Furutsu neighborhood of {{ill|Akiha-ku, Niigata|en|Akiha-ku, Niigata|qid=Q711733|lt=Akiha-ku, Niigata|1=Akiha-ku, Niigata}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2005, with the area under preservation extended in 2011<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173975 |title=古津八幡山遺跡|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 30, 2020}}</ref> It is the northernmost moated Yayoi settlement site thus far discovered on the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|qid=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}} coast.
==Overview== During the late Yayoi period, settlements began appearing on hilltop locations with defensive fortifications. The Furutsu Hachimanyama Site was discovered in 1987 during construction of the {{ill|Ban-etsu Expressway|en|Ban-etsu Expressway|qid=Q1059200|lt=Ban-etsu Expressway|1=Ban-etsu Expressway}}. It consists of a late Yayoi period moated settlement on a slight elevation (15 to 55 meters) above the Niigata Plain in between the {{ill|Shinano River|en|Shinano River|qid=Q734455|lt=Shinano|1=Shinano River}} and {{ill|Agano River|en|Agano River|qid=Q390458|lt=Agano River|1=Agano River}}s, extending for 400 meters from north-to-south and 150 meters from east-to-west. The settlement was surrounded by a V-shaped moat, intermittent in places, double in others, with a depth three meters and width of two meters. The soil excavated from the moats were used to form a rudimentary earthen {{ill|Rampart (fortification)|en|Rampart (fortification)|qid=Q1416685|lt=rampart|1=Rampart (fortification)}}. Within the moat were the foundation pillar holes for 45 rectangular raised-floor dwellings, measuring four to five meters on each side, with a hearth in the center and a drainage groove. The foundations of 32 [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s have also been identified. {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|qid=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery|1=Yayoi pottery}} from the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}}, notably from what is now {{ill|Aizu|en|Aizu|qid=Q1205071|lt=Aizu|1=Aizu}} and other parts of the Hokuriku region have been found, together with locally-made pottery. This indicates some form of long distance trade and a connection between this site and the inland region of Aizu. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Two square tumuli are located a short distance outside the moat, and an iron sword with a deer horn handle and a stone spearpoint were excavated from the traces of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s. A little further away, on a small rise overlooking the settlement is a "two conjoined rectangles" type tumuli (''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}'' (前方後方墳)), orientated to the north, which was found to have no [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s. It is estimated to date from the end of the Yayoi period. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Some artifacts uncovered at the site are on display at the {{nihongo|Yayoi Hill Exhibition Hall|弥生の丘展示館|Yayoi no Oka tenjikan}} at site, and some representations of pit houses have been rebuilt. The site is about a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Furutsu Station|en|Furutsu Station|qid=Q5509827|lt=Furutsu Station|1=Furutsu Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Shin'etsu Main Line|en|Shin'etsu Main Line|qid=Q1038051|lt=Shin'etsu Main Line|1=Shin'etsu Main Line}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
===Furutsu Hachimanyama Kofun=== The {{nihongo|'''Furutsu Hachimanyama Kofun'''|古津八幡山古墳}} within the National Historic Site is a two-tier domed tumuli, with the domed portion on top of a circular base, partially surrounded by a moat. With a diameter of 60 meters, it is one of the largest in Niigata Prefecture. It is believed to have been constructed in the early [[Kofun period]], but has not been excavated. It is located to the northwest of the settlement at the Furutsu Hachimanyama Site. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:古津八幡山遺跡条溝と土塁.JPG|portion of moat and rampart File:古津八幡山遺跡環濠.JPG|Tumuli File:NiigataCityOpenData rekishinohiroba04.jpg|Reconstructed pit houses File:Furutsu Hachiman Museum 201905.jpg|Furutsu Hachiman Museum </gallery>
==See also== ==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.city.niigata.lg.jp/kanko/bunka/rekishi/maibun/kuni_furutsuhachiman/index.html Niigata city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.city.niigata.lg.jp/smph/akiha/shisetsu/yoka/bunka/yayoinookatenzikan.html Akiha-ku, Niigata page]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
